pattern: Matching a stream of batched buy and sell orders is a two-priority-queue problem: a max-heap of resting buys and a min-heap of resting sells, repeatedly settling the best opposing prices in bulk until they no longer cross.
hints: Process the batches in the given order, keeping resting buy orders in a structure that exposes the highest price and resting sell orders in one that exposes the lowest price.

When a buy batch arrives, keep consuming the cheapest resting sell while its price is at most the buy price, decrementing whichever batch runs out first; mirror this for an arriving sell against the most expensive resting buy.
Whatever amount of the incoming batch is left after matching becomes a new resting order; at the end sum every resting amount modulo 1000000007.

edge_inputs.description: first statement example with leftover buys
second statement example exercising the modulo
single buy order with nothing to match
single sell order with nothing to match
buy and sell at equal price fully cancel
sell priced above all buys so nothing matches
incoming buy clears several resting sells of differing prices
partial match leaves part of the same batch resting

stress.description: random batches with small prices so many matches occur
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    orders = []
    for _ in range(n):
        price = rng.randint(1, 50)
        amount = rng.randint(1, 1000000000)
        otype = rng.randint(0, 1)
        orders.append([price, amount, otype])
    return [orders]
stress.note: tight price band forces frequent crossing

stress.description: wide price range so matches are rarer and the backlog grows
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    orders = []
    for _ in range(n):
        price = rng.randint(1, 1000000000)
        amount = rng.randint(1, 1000000000)
        otype = rng.randint(0, 1)
        orders.append([price, amount, otype])
    return [orders]
stress.note: sparse crossings stress the modulo accumulation
